reviews.review_text:
Marvel’s Midnight Suns is a unique and surprisingly deep strategy game that blends Marvel characters with turn-based tactics and card-based gameplay.

The combat is one of the strongest parts of the game. Using cards to control abilities adds a fresh twist, and each character feels different, which keeps battles interesting and strategic.

The roster is also a big highlight. Seeing characters like Iron Man, Blade, Ghost Rider, and others interact is fun, and they all have their own playstyles that make team-building enjoyable.

Outside of combat, the social and relationship systems add another layer to the experience. However, I do wish some parts of the game were more tidied up, as certain systems and interactions can feel a bit messy or not as polished as they could be.

Overall, Marvel’s Midnight Suns is a solid and unique Marvel game with a lot of depth. It’s enjoyable, but it could have been even better with a bit more polish. 

Gameplay: 7/10 Good | Overall: 7/10 Good
I'm having trouble launching the game. Despite multiple attempts, it fails to open. When I click 'Play,' the status changes to 'Running,' but no game window appears on the screen.

If they re released this game without requiring friendships, hangouts, gifting, and tighten up the story, I may play 400 hours. The gameplay and core base buildings great like all x-com. They really took a dump on the sim aspects.

I bought this game recently on a good sale with all the DLC. I should love this game. I'm a big Marvel comics fan who was already intimately familiar with every character featured here. I love games like XCOM or Wasteland, and I love deckbuilders.

My main criticism: THIS GAME DOES NOT RESPECT YOUR TIME.

You will spend most of your playtime trudging through vapid, unfunny, unimportant dialogue, or receiving tutorials on various out-of-combat systems that use a billion different little tokens. It feels like a mobile game in that way. You will also spend a huge amount of time walking around your home base area to get to anything. Compare this to a game like XCOM where the foundry and the research lab are a click away. Imagine if you physically had to walk through an uninteresting environment for about a minute before you could change your research subject.

Tragically, the combat is mostly pretty good. The game just wants you to do absolutely anything but combat. That said, I did tap out when I was in Part 2 of a mission that was functionally unwinnable. The cards you draw are the same cards in the same order on each repeated attempt, so my only option was to restart the entire mission, and even then I'm not sure it would have worked. It was a DLC mission, so I'm not sure how well balanced it was.

There's a good game in there. Unfortunately, they don't really want you to play it, and that's probably because there's not actually that much of it.
solid gameplay and the combat is interesting and fun for a deckbuilding turn based game

One of the best games I've ever played. Might be one of the best Marvel games of all time.

I read there are reviews about the writing and dialogues being bad, but honestly, I don't find it like so.
The story is actually good and engaging enough to make me play everyday and hyped to finish it right after work.

It's like an interactive movie. The graphics, audio and gameplay blends so well.

There are some performance issues with final arena boss fight where using Legendary cards ( [spoiler] especially Iron Man's! [/spoiler] ) eats frames like it's dinner on a Tuesday. I literally have to play in that arena boss fight under 30fps.

[spoiler] Deadpool introduction and dialogues are one of the funniest interaction in video games I've ever experienced. It's a shame his cards are bad (in my opinion). [/spoiler]

The game is not short but not too long either. It's longer than average, and I find it nice.

Honestly, I would gave it 10/10 but for that bad performance alone I would give it -- 

9/10.

Please play it. I hope there's Midnight Suns 2!

slow to start but picks up, gameplay loop is solid. voice acting grew on me after awhile. a shame it was slept on deserves a sequel. anyways one of the better marvel games (ill admit the bar is kinda low there tho). 

best thing? no doomsday clock :)

I want to like this game, but it feels like they adjusted a bunch of stuff to make microtransactions more attractive. Literally the entire time you're in the home base part of the game it feels like they're trying to get you to drop money.

Richard Garfield, creator of Magic the Gathering, describes games like this as "Skinnerware". Machines designed to take your money, not designed to be fun and entertaining first.

The actual game part of the game, like the battles and mechanics and that kind of stuff is actually pretty neat.

So much about this game hits, but overall the story stuff really drags, to the point I have a hard time enjoying it. There is a great combat system here, and I can tolerate some Marvel shenanigans, but if you are going to make me sit through this much cringe writing I'm sorry I just can't do it.

A gem of a game i did not expect to be so worth it. While the complete bundle is drastically overpriced (recommend buying dlc on sale, but are must haves). The gameplay, while seeming boring over the ads, is actually quite addicting and compliment the semi-RPG element portions of the game. Base game is worth full price and exploring! Dlc are overpriced sadly. Have played on both Ps5 and PC, and still love this game
Incredible game. They do service to the characters. The dialogue feels authentic. The combat mechanics are innovative and engaging. I wish it was 50 times longer so I could spend a decade playing it.

I have yet to beat the game. My review is for what I've experienced so far. I enjoy the story along with the characters interactions and the individuals themselves. the character models are very good and attention to detail is definitely noted with some. I highly recommend this game to anyone that enjoys a Marvel version of Xcom with card elements.

The game is not bad. It's a unique take on turn based combat I haven't seen before.
The performance was horrendous in the hub area and in some battles.
While not strictly necessary to do, maxing relations with the heroes was way too much of a pain.

Overall it’s surprisingly fun and charming. 
It seems like a western take of the Persona games…

Combat has good depth. It’s a tactical turn-based deck builder. I enjoy trying out new builds or new team comps and I appreciate the option to restart combat when things go south…

Free time is friendship building + free exploration. Friendship story feels like high school drama, which made sense to me since the game is Marvel Persona. I quite liked the mini open world - it is not overwhelming (it’s a tiny area map after all) and is a fun change of pace with lore.

I started for the gameplay and really didn’t care for the story at first, and yes it’s corny at times, but it grew on me - coming from someone who is not familiar with comic or MCU at all. Some characters are very likable. Regardless, if you enjoy tactical combat, the game is worth considering for gameplay alone.

Very fun combat, good story and a lot of dialog if you do the friendship stuff, which gets kinda too much imo. The game ran well except for some stutters, but the Endboss fight was laggy af, idk who optimzed it but damn. Still a good game overall.

If you like deck builders, this is very fun. The scaling is rewarding, the friendship levels are fun to explore with good rewards for maxing them out, I wish there was more content to get but it's rewarding nonetheless.
Excellent turn based fighting game with obvious extra appeal to Marvel fans, not a bad story so far (about 40 hours in)

This is an excellent title, it has turn based card gameaplay and is very strategic. The graphics are excellent and the production values quite high. You get to create your own character which has his own back story and personality. A lot of dialog options that affect the gameplay. Lots of stuff to do and upgrade with tons of voiced dialog with the characters etc. I loved that its mostly card based gameplay with quite a bit of destructible environments and objects you can literally throw at the enemies etc. You can spend a lot of time running around and exploring the area and finding secrets and areas to take other characters to etc. Would have liked more DLC with other famous characters but what you get is still quite a lot. This is one of the most unexpected and best type of games from Marvel.

[h1]Fundamentally, this game is a Visual Novel. And even there it comes up short.[/h1]

Everyone of my gripes boils down to this. They focused on making sure every character has a comment on every story or mission beat. It's actually rather impressive. But clearly they did not have the resources to put this level of attention in other aspects of the game. Even here at the "visual novel" aspect I find infuriating lacking.

There is so much wrong at this game at it's core. There is a huge cast of characters and yet the main story only really focuses on 4 of them, not including your character. The deck building is clunky, and it sucks to make tweaks to a deck for a specific mission or team combo and instead you just make generalized isolated "good" decks to swap in and out for convivence. Characters lack deep synergy, and are only barely loosely connected. The game feels like it was meant to be a F2P Gacha game with how you have to "roll" for cards and artifacts. Even the main focus of this game, the dialogue, feels horrible at times. Some characters are betrayals of themselves, or Flanderizations of themselves. Tony specifically feels utterly insufferable. Idk if this is Fireaxis fundamentally not understanding the characters, the issue of having too many hero's, or if Marvel demanded certain things from these characters.

That's not even saying how jittery this game is. I feel I have a decent PC, but on Steam Deck and on my PC it stutters even on medium. It feels poorly optimized, like they rushed it out half baked. This feeling is compounded with how a character doesn't even get a Bond Rank or bond time, even in New Game +.

What is the final nail for me is that this game is $80 for everything, and you can see the remnants of where they tried to microtransaction the cosmetics. For what this game is, and assuming it was abandoned like this as Firaxis moved on, that is utterly shameful.  I got this game on sale for $20, and I feel I barely got my worth out of it.

I could go on, but I am deeply disappointed in Fireaxis. I hope this was some weird outside influence working with Marvel that caused this game to flop. I hope they recognize that too, since apparently they said the sales for this game were underwhelming. I hope this isn't going to be a pattern from one of my favorite game studios.
Really really enjoy this game. It's a shame they are not doing more season passes, feels like a missed chance having a Midnight Suns game without Moonknight in it. Please make more games like this!

Never actually managed to finish the game.

It’s not bad, just... meh.

The actual gameplay got really repetitive towards the second half, and it was pretty simplistic to begin with. That bothered me because turn-based strategies, and turn-based games in general, are my #1 most-played category.

The social aspects are only fun if you are a big Marvel fan.

That said, I didn’t bother writing a review until I noticed the new Denuvo checks, so I might as well.

Do not care about the corpo reviews, I waited until this game was 9.99 to show my disinterest before attempting it, and I was correct. You had the opportunity to make one of the greatest ARPG's known to gaming and you chose to throw it away to do something no one asked for. This game was a commercial flop because no one wanted to control marvel super hero's with trading cards. That is the dumbest game design decision made in the last two decades. Take the most action based franchise on the planet and reduce it to Magic: The Gathering. The people who made this decision I hope moved to their true calling in finance software design and left gaming for the gamers. Someone please make a Marvel ARPG that we'll actually want to play.
Story is good, only redeeming quality.
Fun gameplay. Great writing and voice acting. Needs optimizing in the most dire way.
horrendous graphics, but decent diologue and a fun combat system.
great card game! pretty bad and not fun other suff

So let me get this straight. The impulse to write this review was the news of publisher implementing additional intrusive checks years post launch, supposedly requiring an online connection every 14 days. I didn't want to just repeat possible misinfo on the internet, though, I needed to verify this was a problem. Folks, 14 days is an overestimate. I took my Steam Deck completely offline between activation runs so no OS or game upgrade could disrupt the authentication state (I'm assuming shutdowns wouldn't disrupt it, because come on) and I was lucky when the game lasted 3 days without telling me it needs online mode for the "first launch". Then again, sometimes it straight crashed on startup. One could reasonably blame the hardware configuration, but this was exclusively a startup thing.

The rumour goes, this is a response to copyright infringers managing a way around protection in the earlier version of the game. Now they have possibly a better experience playing it. Brilliant. This would be enough to recommend avoiding this game. But... maybe you don't care about that?

I have no big complaints about technical side of actual game running, or the quality of voice acting. The other elements of the game, though, are weird, and not in the best sense. The writing has this stereotypical sheen of artificiality associated with Marvel movies, and the graphical style feels somewhat odd to me, though I can imagine someone liking them both. The two really questionable elements are primary stage gameplay and the overall upgrade system between stages, which are pretty big parts of the game, alright.

The combat stages use a card based system, and I'd like to emphasize I don't have a problem with shuffled cards being a part of a combat game. However, their particular implementation feels very unnatural to me. The characters don't have separate decks, so conversation of ninjutsu starts applying. A lot of cards kind of ignore the physical locations of characters and have them run up to arbitrary enemies in a stage - often to unintuitive distance from unintuitive direction - but many non-card prop-based moves and a few cards do rely on directions derived from characters' positions to work. It isn't really coherent in my opinion. I wouldn't exactly call this system engaging, but at worst it's an unsuccessful experiment; it's passable.

The upgrade loop, on the other hand, feels much more like an intended problem. It has multiple types of lootboxes available for finishing missions, destructive crafting of cards into better "normal" cards and one-time perishable items, resource starvation on the default difficulty level very early into the game. It feels very much like a free-to-play mobile title, except the menu interface was replaced with walking and someone added picking mushrooms and such to make it less obvious. I can only imagine this has something to do with the recent push for an online connection. I cannot check it at the moment - the game is crashing on online startup (while refusing to launch "for the first time" offline). I honestly don't know which option is worse - that this fairly expensive title with paid DLC asks for further money to skip the grind, or that it doesn't.

Cherry on top, the writing's scripting feels like it's falling apart despite also feeling very rudimentary. I'm fairly certain one non-trivial event (Dr Strange being shown a red seal thing) happened multiple times to me. I can accept this game being less ambitious in regards to intercharacter interactione than the competition, but at least they could not bug it up that much...

Do not buy this game in its current state.

I proudly recommend this game. Here's what I think:

the story is really good, and the gameplay is pretty good

Bought this game for 9 bucks on sale, definitely worth it at that price. My biggest complaint about this game is having to explore the abbey grounds, which can get tedious and boring quick. The story is okay, it's not too deep but it's good enough. The combat is where this game shines: there are so many different combinations and synergies. I do wish that there were more cards though, especially for heroes that are not the main character. I wasn't a fan of the ending mission but again, for 9 bucks? I can't really complain. I would not buy this game at full price. Wait for a sale.

Very interesting mix of deckbuilding and RPG elements with the wild world of Marvel. 

Even outside of the combat there is a lot of fun with exploring the Abbey between missions and just overhearing so much or finding neat puzzles and quests around.

While some of the characters can get a bit annoying for the sake of team drama, there is a ton of amazing comic campiness mixed with those moments that keep me going. Always welcome to see Robbie get more love, and seeing unique interactions with teams built up also can be hilarious with talk and crazy results in game. Been having a blast with it, wish this got more love and exposure could have been great to see better expansions or even a sequel. But enjoyed this a ton either way!
Goofy dialogue at times, but the core game is quality and challenging.

its fun back then but lately there is performance issue on the training yard keep dropping fps. grinding friendship can be annoying with random night hangout. unless you bring your main character to bond with the heroes you like.
If you are looking for longer like x-com gameplay this is probably not the best to look. its a short game otherwise, once you get every card min maxing its start feeling repetitive.

Midnight Suns is a surprisingly heartfelt strategy RPG that hooked me more than I expected. The core gameplay blends story-driven RPG elements with Slay the Spire-style card combat, and it works really well - battles are strategic, satisfying, and never feel stale. Outside of combat, building friendships with your team is a big part of the experience, and that’s where the game really shines. The bonds and banter between characters feel genuine, even if the dialogue can get a bit corny and the voice acting occasionally leans into over-the-top cheese.

The story itself is solid, and more importantly, I actually found myself caring about what was going on, which is rare for me with games like this. There’s a lot of heart here, even though the devs felt like they were handed a list of directives by DEI consultants. Still, the overall package comes together in a way that’s engaging, unique, and easy to get invested in. A pleasant surprise and a really fun spin on the Marvel universe. But then, I was always a fan of the Midnight Sons in the comics, as rarely as they appear.
Love the gameplay and the lore always nice to learn more about the marvel universe

Decent tactics game made better by a whole host of characters and really good effects (even if they take awhile sometimes). Excellent voice acting with some hit or miss dialogue, but that's any Marvel thing tbh

This game is solid. The open world exploration bits aren’t as fun as they should be, and hanging out with your favorite hero’s isn’t quite as fun as it should be. The gameplay is pretty solid, a combination of deck builder and grid based strategy, and all of it with a marvel skin. It’s… fine. I didn’t finish it, I just got bored after 25ish hours. I set it down and never came back, and never will. If it’s on a big sale it’s probably worth it.

Excellent game, mixing some fun (if repetitive) gameplay with some good background storytelling. 

Unfortunately it hits a pet peeve of mine: if I find a bug, google it and then find that it's been common for years without being fixed I'm always disappointed, and this game is full of them, especially in New Game +

Overall I recommend this, but only at sale price

UPDATE: I am changing this review for positive only because from my world perspective a person can't leave negative review on a game one spent 43 hours in and keeps playing it. But everything bellow is still valid with one exception - core gameplay is developing greatly through the game progress and i enjoyed it a lot. That's the reason for 43 hours.

When I heard "marvel game from XCOM team" I was sold immediately, but the truth is, it is extremely questionable. 
It tries to be so much and fails miserably at everything so in the end we get a mixed-genre abomination with mechanics from everything that just doesn't work.

It feels like a game planned to be a F2P Mobile Card Gacha, that for some reason switched into a PC singleplayer. The game introduces tons of meta-mechanics like relations, training, resource-gathering, base decorations etc, etc, etc. But all of that is to compensate for the lack of deep core gameplay.

Core gameplay design is... acceptable. I was honestly expecting something xcom-like, but I guess the idea to make Marvel game less tactical and more puzzlish works towards more broad audience (not like XCOM has a small audience, but super-hero theme can certainly attract lots of kids and casual players), I can't really comment much on it as it feels like I have lots more to try with it.

What I really want to comment on is EVERYTHING ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ELSE! The pacing of the game is terrible. They tried to go hard CRPG with HOURS of dialogues and killed any motivation on exploring those dialogues. So we have casual gameplay with BG3 dialogues and a relationship system. You think it's weird enough? Wait, there's more. How about lootboxes? And MORE LOOTBOXES? [b]AND EVEN MORE LOOTBOXES[/b]? There are at least 3 types of those. And you can't just get the rewards after a mission. The gameplay loop forces you to WALK between different locations to do everything. Imagine if the base at XCOM was a 1:1 replica where you have to walk on foot to do everything you usually just clicked. Sounds terrible? Not to MMS designers apparently. 

The game doesn't appreciate your time at all.
You want to speed up animation? No. You want to skip the lootbox opening animation you saw 50 times already? No. You want to read dialogues and not wait for them to be voiced to you? Well. Not always. 50% of dialogues are cutscenes where you can either skip it whole or watch it at a 1x pace.


As a Summary:
Is it an overall bad game? - No. It can be fun and the core gameplay feels fresh, even though it doesn't play like XCOM at all.
Can I recommend it? - Honestly, no. Playing it feels like being forced to eat 7 appetisers that were made by a motel cafe cook to have a lick of a main course cooked by a master-chef. Not worth it.
In my opinion a very overlooked game. Its sad that we might never get an official sequel for this.
the story is not great and the portrayals of characters can be pretty insufferable, but the gameplay is pretty neat. at least, the main gameplay. idk what the whole abbey thing is cooking.

The start of the game is pretty rough, and Marvel's Midnight Suns is sometimes too ambitious for its own good, but honestly, after a few hours, you start to see the beauty in all those systems, the poetry in all those conversations, the consitance in the whole game. I have a great time playing both story and side missions. The game also captures the voice of Marvel characters wonderfully, and the tactical depth is here. Give it a try, you won't regret it!
Super fun game. Dialogue could be better but the gameplay makes it worth for it. If you like strategy with card building and Marvel, it is a must play.

The card based combat in this game is some of the best I have ever played!  It has great variety in card types, the combo cards between heroes are powerful and impactful and the mechanics of combat in general are enjoyable and easy to learn.  The paths for upgrading, and gaining new, cards is related to rewards and resources gained from doing missions, which adds to the team building elements of the missions, along with level upgrades and story progression.

The story is extremely interesting and different to the mainstream Marvel offerings we have come to expect with the addition of a much deeper arcane story and gameplay.

The only downside to the game can be the interactions between the heroes in the Abbey, between missions.  Much of the banter, and a fair amount of the discussion lines, can be a little juvenile, but they do flesh out the back stories of the heroes and increase the bonds of friendship, in turn increasing the amount of available combos and their strength.  There is also some light resource gathering and crafting elements centered around the Abbey as well.

All in all, I have been enjoying this game MUCH more than I expected to.  I hope there is a sequel one day, or at least another entry utilising the 'Midnight Suns' team.
Definitely recommended for deck builder game players! for people new to this kind of games, they need to practice it a bit to understand the game mechanics and other stufff.

dude if you get any dlc for this game get the deadpool one they did him so right in this game and plus we need more games with ghost rider in it, sadly so far im pretty sure they only have danny reyes ghost rider and I prefer johnny cash ghost rider but ill take what I can get
I liked the story. I wish upgrading cards was a little easier but overall this game was very fun to play. To bad it will not have a part 2

It's hard for me to put more than 40 hours in a game.
Midnight Suns is an excelent game where you can play one or two missions and then close the game.

Marvel XCOM - The Tactical Card Game is actually a pretty good game. It's not amazing, not a classic, but altogether it gets it's point across, has some very inspired moments and even good graphics. The low points are that the game is buggy and nobody will try to fix it anymore - it really does feel abandoned sometimes.

Do I recommend it fully? Yeah, but buy it at a discount. 
Also, the DLC characters are kinda fun.

Friendship Simulator 2000, the best part of the game is genuinely the relationships with all the heroes, they're endearing, well written and some of the interpersonal stuff really made me laugh. 

The fighting is fun and can be decently challenging, but it's fairly samey after a while.

Probably not worth the full price, but getting it on sale is well worth it.
Best Marvel game ever! You truly feel like a superhero and part of the extended universe!

This is an incredibly solid card battler. It's a real shame that this had to be a Marvel game - if people weren't so fatigued with the brand this could have been a more successful title. At the very least, it should have gotten a sequel.

A lot of annoying stuff.

overall pretty fun though.  The relationship stuff is annoying. Finding hidding things with no markers is annoying.
Not a great game.  When the cinematics started I felt like I was back in 2003.  combat is boring even for a card game.

Surprising little game that I enjoyed. Marvel, missions, and some light exploration of the grounds. Each character feels unique and viable and the dialogue is enjoyable, like watching a B tier Marvel movie. Plenty of game and worth it.

definitely a buy when it is on sale for under ten dollars. especially for super hero nerds like myself. but it is not worth full price so far and kind of reminds me too much of a AAA flop to be categorized as worth 60 bucks. but for less than ten bucks it's definitely been fun thus far.

All perfect arts, spectacular designs and graphics, my all beloved heroes... But for what?  A useless card game? Really i can't believe Marvel's bad choices, it might be a full adventure game, not a lame card game. And tutorials are very bad, useless. Why can't redraw even when heroic levels full. Where is tips? Second tutorial drove me crazy and i uninstalled game. Don't purchase, doesn't worth it.

I just cant get into it.  I dont know why its a tactical turn based with a card system.  Why would I have all these super heroes who can only use random abilities.  Why is all the conversations feel like a team of millenials pulled the dialogue from teen drama romance books, and why is every character so flamboyant?  I really wanted to like and enjoy this game but it feels like the developer wanted to make a marvel SIMS game and threw in the combat as an after thought.  It just is not good.

One of the rare games that I buy on multiple systems:D The friendship mechanic and exploring the Abbey is so fun; especially Charlie <33333333333333333

Nico has become one of my favorite Marvel characters after this and the Midnight Suns got me back into the comics!
Amazing story, graphics and missions

In short: yes if you like grinding with your fav marvel chars and are prepared for a horrible unsatisfactory ending. Personally I thoroughly enjoyed it. Not even the great Jennifer Hale voice acting Lilith can save that terrible ending. Just have Deadpool voiced by Nolan North in every mission and you will have fun. Simple as that.

Pros and Reasons to try it:
- It has Deadpool, Iron Man, Cap, Blade, Logan, Magik, Venom (best voiced and character infusion)
- Surprisingly strategic combat, but not overwhelmingly so
- Commander Shepard (Femshep) voices the female villain
- Deadpool is the best voiced character, the most fun to have in any party, brightens your day every time. His story arc up to LV5 friendship has more fun dialogue than 1/3 of the total roaster. Facts.


Cons and Reasons to avoid:
- Grindy missions. You need to grind to level up your abilities and reputation.
- Cringy dialogues from many characters, some rather terrible cutscenes, and long-drawn dialogues that do nothing but inflated the time, at times seems like a horrible korean soap opera than a marvel game.
- Femshep villain has an abysmal ending. I wont spoil it for you, but basically everything you have been doing just reverses in the last fight mission with new information that seems so badly designed that the entire writers department should have been fired. 
- Deadpool is severely underpowered compared to other DLC chars like Storm.
- Difficulty spikes. You can destroy some fights in tougher difficulties, but get annihilated in others, which forces you to bring your A team.
- Many characters like Nico are abnoxious. Others like Wanda have the male body type in casual clothes (she literally has Cap's body size).
- If you pick male character (or the body size A or B whatever the crap they put in as politically correct) HIS animations will be based on FEMALE animations. Basically, regardless which gender you pick, there is only one single animation blueprint, which unfortunately is female-based. Which is badly designed to have a Dark-inclined Hunter protagonist with Devil horns and dark armor, to run or stand like a damsel in distress. Wanda on the other hand, when in her casual form in the abbey, stands like a freaking tower of masculinity. Im not sure what was the thought process there. Mass Effect did this perfectly 15 years ago, both male and female animations seemed on point.
The combat was way too repetitive and slow-paced.  Story is meh.  It wasn't absolutely terrible, but there are a ton of other games I'd rather waste my time playing.

comic books, like all mediums, are mostly awful, poorly written schlock. in the average comic characters act stupidly, don't talk through anything, and mostly just bumble around to the tune called by whatever hack writer has been deposited into the title through this week's nepotism lottery. it is rare for one to transcend this, to have actual depth of emotion and characters worth partaking in the story and struggles of. this game is in the first category. this is not a watchmen type game. this is not a final crisis type game. ♥♥♥♥, this isn't even a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ kraven's last hunt type game. this is a weekly serial "nothing ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ happens and everyone's a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ moron" type game. don't buy this. have some ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ self respect.
